title: CSS Pseudo-elements
description: Create decorative elements and style specific parts of content with pseudo-elements.

lessons.title: The ::before Element
lessons.task: Add a bullet before each list item using <kbd>::before</kbd> with <kbd>content: "• "</kbd>.
lessons.previewBaseCSS: body { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 1rem; } .list { list-style: none; padding: 0; margin: 0; } .list li { padding: 8px 0; }

lessons.title: Styling ::before
lessons.previewBaseCSS: body { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 1rem; } .list { list-style: none; padding: 0; margin: 0; } .list li { padding: 8px 0; } .list li::before { content: "• "; }

lessons.title: The ::after Element
lessons.description:
::after works like ::before but inserts content as the last child. Common uses include badges, icons, or decorative elements.

.new::after {
  content: " ✓";
  color: green;
}
lessons.task: Add a checkmark after completed items with <kbd>content: " ✓"</kbd>.
lessons.previewHTML:
• Buy groceries
  • Walk the dog
  • Read a book
lessons.previewBaseCSS: body { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 1rem; } .list { list-style: none; padding: 0; margin: 0; } .list li { padding: 8px 0; }

lessons.title: Decorative Lines
lessons.task: Create an underline decoration with <kbd>width: 40px</kbd>, <kbd>height: 3px</kbd>, and <kbd>background: steelblue</kbd>.
lessons.previewBaseCSS: body { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 1rem; } .title { margin: 0 0 1rem; } .title::after { content: ""; display: block; margin-top: 8px; } p { margin: 0; color: #666; }
